reference.title: Glossary
reference.shortdesc: Definitions of key terms used throughout this guide.

dlentry.dd: A set of rules and protocols that allows software applications to communicate with each other. In the context of this guide, API refers to web APIs that communicate over HTTP.
A Postman container that groups related API requests, folders, test scripts, and documentation. Collections are the primary unit of organization in Postman.
A Postman feature that executes all requests in a collection in sequence, automating the test suite and reporting aggregated pass/fail results.

A named set of key-value variable pairs in Postman. Switching the active environment changes the variable values resolved in all requests, enabling the same collection to run against different systems.
A specific URL path on an API server that responds to HTTP requests. For example, /users/{id} is an endpoint that returns a user record.
A variable defined in a Postman environment. Referenced in requests using double curly brace syntax: {{variableName}}.
A verb that indicates the action a request should perform on a resource. Common methods include GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, and DELETE.
A simulated API server in Postman that returns predefined responses. Used to test integrations before the real API is available.
JavaScript code in Postman that runs before a request is sent. Used to set variables, generate tokens, or modify request parameters programmatically.
A key-value pair appended to a URL after the ? character. For example, in /users?role=admin, the query parameter is role=admin.
Data sent with a request, typically used with POST, PUT, and PATCH methods. Postman supports JSON, form-data, URL-encoded, and binary body types.
The data returned by the API server after processing a request. A response includes a status code, headers, and optionally a body.
A three-digit number returned in the HTTP response that indicates the result of the request. For example, 200 means success and 404 means the resource was not found.
JavaScript code written in the Tests tab of a Postman request that runs after a response is received to assert expected values and validate API behavior.
A named placeholder used in Postman requests and scripts. Variables are referenced using the {{variableName}} syntax and resolved at runtime from the active environment, collection, or global scope.
A collaborative space in Postman that contains collections, environments, APIs, and mock servers. Workspaces can be personal or shared with a team.
